mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production directly tracks aggregate output and corporate revenue; persistent expansion in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns because higher output translates into earnings surprises.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in less than 55% of months with rising INDPRO, or Spearman correlation between INDPRO change and 20d return is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% represents a restrictive monetary stance that raises borrowing costs and equity risk premiums; once this threshold is crossed, the S&P 500 exhibits negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20d returns in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales reflect synchronized labor income and consumption demand expansion; this joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns because it indicates broad-based economic momentum that single-series signals cannot capture.
candidate_trigger: Joint 1-month increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce higher positive returns than the best single-series signal alone (Sharpe ratio difference < 0.2), or the interaction term in a bivariate regression has a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: In restrictive monetary regimes where the federal funds rate exceeds 5.5%, sustained elevation in the policy rate depresses present values of long-duration cash flows and triggers equity derating, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60d returns are not negative in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.5%, or the mean return conditional on this regime is not statistically below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative 0.3 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over three months signals deteriorating labor demand and impending consumption weakness; this threshold crossing predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets rapidly price in recession risk.
failure_condition: Negative 5d returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the hit rate of negative returns is not significantly above the unconditional baseline (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An annualized inflation rate proxied by CPIAUCSL exceeding 3.5% erodes real corporate margins and pressures the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy; this inflation threshold predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60d returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with a federal funds rate below 3.0% identifies an accommodative policy environment supporting real output growth; this interaction predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns because low financing costs amplify the earnings impact of expanding production.
failure_condition: The joint condition does not outperform a simple INDPRO-only signal (information ratio difference < 0.15), or returns conditional on the joint signal are not positive in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeding 0.8% signals an above-trend demand environment that supports earnings revisions; this threshold predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns occur in fewer than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 QoQ growth exceeds 0.8%.
